title: rudgea citrifolia
intro: Rudgea citrifolia, also known as the Lemon Bitter, is a plant species that belongs to the Rubiaceae family. It is a shrub native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, and its leaves and bark have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties. Rudgea citrifolia contains alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antioxidant properties, contributing to its potential health benefits, such as treating fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of Rudgea citrifolia can be found in tea, infusions, and extracts, and are often used in traditional medicine to alleviate various health conditions.
